extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won atop Orcières-Merlette after KUSS paced relentlessly inside 2km, neutralizing all attacks and delivering his leader for the stage win.
tactical_patterns: KUSS sustained tempo inside 2km cracked KWIATKOWSKI immediately, demonstrating high-altitude pacing neutralizes late attacks more effectively than following wheels.
MARTIN Guillaume attacked inside 300m but ROGLIČ closed solo, suggesting late-stage accelerations on summit finishes require 500m+ gaps to survive.
SAGAN boxed himself in intermediate sprint chasing wheels instead of positioning early, costing him green jersey points to BENNETT.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: delivered devastating pace inside 2km that immediately dropped KWIATKOWSKI, exceptional high-altitude domestique work
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: closed MARTIN's 300m attack solo and won, showing superior late-race acceleration on summit finish
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: negative: cracked immediately when KUSS raised tempo inside 2km, unexpectedly poor climbing form
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: positive: strong enough to attack inside 300m on summit finish, confirming French expectations as dark horse
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: fourth on stage in comeback, showing renewed climbing form after recent struggles
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: faded to fifth but retained yellow with same time, revealing sprint punch insufficient against true climbers on summit finishes
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: finished 12th at intermediate sprint after poor wheel selection, tactical error cost green jersey points
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: gained crucial intermediate sprint points over SAGAN through superior positioning, tactical discipline paying dividends
